Location Focus

Curated Scope

How the studio tends to shape work in Chorlton.

01

Stone and tile

Graphic surface treatments are handled with clean set-out and disciplined contrast.

02

Joinery detail

Carpentry and trim choices hold the composition together.

03

Premium process

Site work is sequenced to protect finish quality instead of chasing speed.

Selected Project

Graphic stone-detail renovation

A darker material scheme where the success of the project depended on line quality, contrast and careful interface detailing.

Stone-led palette High-contrast finish Craft-focused execution

FAQ

Questions we usually answer before a Chorlton brief starts.

Can darker palettes still feel premium and calm?

Yes. With the right line work and material balance, darker schemes feel composed rather than heavy.

Do you advise on contrast and metals?

Yes. Metal tone, stone value and trim detail are all part of the design phase.

Is Chorlton in direct coverage?

Yes. It sits within the core coverage pattern already shown across the site.
